mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a confirmed cyclical earnings recovery, driving institutional capital inflows into equities and generating positive forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 40% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold being crossed.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with stabilizing consumer credit balances indicate broad-based demand recovery, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 2% month-over-month while RSAFS stabilizes
failure_condition: The joint housing-credit signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no predictive power for forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Deep contractions in industrial production trigger mean-reversion dynamics in manufacturing output, which historically precede short-term equity bounces as supply constraints ease and capacity utilization rebounds.
failure_condition: SPX continues declining for more than 30 trading days after industrial production crosses below the 90 threshold, contradicting the mean-reversion premise.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly accelerations in real GDP growth reduce recession probabilities and compress equity risk premiums, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no statistical correlation with GDP growth acceleration over 60-day windows, and the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month inflation forces market participants to price in tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite consecutive inflation spikes, indicating that inflation does not trigger the expected multiple compression.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth coinciding with declining unemployment confirms labor market tightening, which drives cyclical equity outperformance as wage growth supports consumer spending and corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100 thousand while UNRATE falls > 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive SPX returns in walk-forward tests, with the average forward return falling below the market median.

mechanism_narrative: Housing market bottoms during deep downturns precede broad economic recovery, as residential investment acts as a leading indicator for consumer confidence and subsequent equity outperformance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of housing bottom instances, demonstrating that housing does not lead equity recovery in this regime.
